landing.heroSubtitle: AI-powered skin lesion analysis connecting rural patients across India with expert urban dermatologists.
landing.howItWorksSubtitle: From photo to consultation in four simple steps
landing.step1Desc: Take a clear photo of your skin lesion and upload it securely from anywhere.
landing.step2Desc: Our hybrid AI model analyzes the image and provides instant classification with confidence scores.
landing.step3Desc: A verified dermatologist reviews your AI report and provides professional guidance.
landing.step4Desc: Connect face-to-face with your doctor via Google Meet for a full consultation.
landing.doctorsSubtitle: Verified specialists ready to help you
landing.locatorSubtitle: Locate verified dermatologists near you — even without an appointment
landing.featuresSubtitle: Designed for patients across rural and urban India
landing.featureAiDesc: Advanced hybrid model trained on 10,000+ dermoscopic images
landing.featureBookingDesc: Schedule appointments in minutes, get instant Google Meet links
landing.featureRuralDesc: Works on 2G/3G with offline support. Available in Hindi and Marathi
landing.featureMultilingualDesc: Full support for English, Hindi, and Marathi — your language
landing.featureRecordsDesc: Your health data encrypted and only accessible by you and your doctor
landing.featureVerifiedDesc: Every doctor is manually verified by our medical team before approval
landing.disclaimer: DERMA AI is for preliminary screening only. It is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

tips.sunscreen_body: Use SPF 30+ sunscreen every day, even on cloudy days. Reapply every 2 hours when outdoors.
tips.abcde_body: Check moles for: Asymmetry, Border irregularity, Color variation, Diameter > 6mm, and Evolution (change over time).
tips.hydration_body: Drink 8+ glasses of water daily. Proper hydration keeps your skin healthy and helps detect abnormalities earlier.
tips.checkup_body: Schedule a full-body skin exam with a dermatologist every year, especially if you have a family history of skin cancer.
tips.diet_body: Tomatoes, carrots, green tea, and nuts contain antioxidants that protect skin cells from damage.
tips.hat_body: Wide-brimmed hats and UV-protective clothing can block up to 98% of harmful UV rays.
tips.mole_body: Photograph your moles monthly and compare changes. Any new or changing spot should be checked by a dermatologist.
